# REGIME READ — 2026-06-18 (Post-FOMC, Pre-Open)

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 2 → 3** (Mild bullish/grind → Neutral/choppy). The previous read's "single binary in 6 hours" resolved hawkish; the regime shifted out of Stage 1 promotion territory toward Stage 3-4 territory. VIX 18.44 still inside Stage 3 range (15-22), not yet Stage 4.
- **Total score: +4 → -1 (delta -5).** Macro lens dropped from -1 to -2 (hawkish dots, 2Y year-high). Dealer Flow dropped from +1 to -1 (vol crush did NOT occur; VXN held 28.56 and VIX spiked +12.37%). Breadth dropped from +1 to 0 (broad-front decline Jun 17). Sentiment from 0 to -1 (F&G 41.29 → 39.5, drifted further into Fear). Geopolitical +2 and Cross-Asset +1 held.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ daily close above $84.59:** **NOT FIRED.** Estimated Jun 17 close ~$77.7 (derived from NDX -0.99%; precise TQQQ close not retrievable). CLEARED as failed.
  - **Bullish — VXN crush to 18-22:** **NOT FIRED — went OPPOSITE.** VXN closed 28.56 (+5.97% Jun 17) vs 26.95 prior intraday. Crush did not occur because the FOMC outcome was the bearish-binary realization.
  - **Bullish — FOMC dot plot held flat or dovish:** **FAILED.** Median 2026 dot raised from 3.4% to 3.8% (~+40bp). 9 of 18 officials project at least one 2026 hike, 6 see multiple hikes. Easing bias stripped from statement. Warsh declined to submit own dot. CLEARED as failed.
  - **Bullish — Iran MOU formally signed Jun 19:** **STILL ARMED on track.** VP Vance + Iran Speaker Ghalibaf signing in Geneva tomorrow per CSIS, Global Guardian, Al Jazeera, NBC, CNN. 60-day ceasefire framework with Hormuz reopened.
  - **Bullish — F&G breaks 45:** **NOT FIRED.** 39.5 (drifted lower from 41.29). CLEARED as failed.
  - **Bearish — FOMC hawkish surprise (+2 or more dots):** **FIRED.** Median dot revision is roughly equivalent (+25bp at end-2026 above current range) plus stripped easing bias plus 9-of-18 see hikes. This is the bearish-binary scenario.
  - **Bearish — TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39:** **ARMED, ON THE EDGE.** Estimated Jun 17 close ~$77.7 → only ~+1.7% cushion above $76.39 (down from +4.9% Jun 16, +10.7% Jun 15). One ordinary down day from triggering.
  - **Bearish — VIX above 22 sustained:** **NOT YET FIRED.** VIX 18.44 (+12.37% Jun 17 — large spike but inside 15-22 range). ARMED.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **NOT FIRED.** 278 bp (Jun 2026, ICE BofA H0A0HYM2 / FRED — modest widening from 271 bp Jun 12). Credit holding.
  - **Bearish — Israel re-engagement pre-Jun 19:** **NOT FIRED.** Israel halted attacks; ceasefire holding into Geneva signing day.
  - **Bearish — Trump escalation / tariff surprise:** **NOT FIRED.** No fresh tariff action Jun 17-18. Section 122 tariffs (post-IEEPA SCOTUS ruling) remain in place under appeal; expiry Jul 24 unless extended.
- **Notable lens flips:** Macro -1 → -2; Dealer Flow +1 → -1; Breadth +1 → 0; Sentiment 0 → -1; Cross-Asset and Geopolitical held.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 3 — Neutral/choppy** (with Stage 4 trigger ARMED; further deterioration possible if 50DMA breaks or VIX sustains >22).
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 17, 2026 — Warsh's debut FOMC delivered hawkish dot escalation; ~+5-pt regime reset (+4 → -1 total).
- **Position:** Day 1 of Stage 3. Three near-term binaries remaining inside the 5-day window: Philly Fed Jun 18 today (forecast +11.4 vs -0.4 prior), MOU formal Geneva signing Jun 19, quad-witching Jun 19.
- **Confidence: Medium.** Three lenses bearish or neutralized, two held bullish (Cross-Asset, Geopolitical), two are neutral-or-negative. Credit holding and Iran-relief intact keep the read from sliding to Stage 4.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2):** TQQQ daily close back above $80 AND VXN crushes through 22 AND a clean MOU signing Jun 19 + benign Philly Fed today AND no follow-on hawkish Fed-speak in next 5 sessions.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39 (only -1.7% from estimated spot), OR VIX sustains above 22 for 2+ sessions, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR Israel re-engagement or MOU collapse pre-Geneva, OR back-to-back hawkish data prints (hot Philly Fed AND low jobless claims AND hot subsequent CPI/PCE).

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment is **Stage 3 neutral-choppy after the hawkish FOMC repriced the cycle**; Medium confidence; **macro headwind is now decisively negative, credit and geopolitics still cushion the downside, and TQQQ sits one ordinary down day from breaking the 50DMA gate.**

## Seven-Lens Scorecard
| Lens | Score | Direction | Key Data (with dates) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Positioning | **0** | Neutral | F&G **39.5 Jun 17** (down from 41.29 Jun 16) — drifted lower in Fear zone, still no Neutral break; NAAIM/AAII/COT/ETF flow detail not retrievable |
| 2. Dealer / Options Flow | **-1** | Bearish | **VIX 18.44 (+12.37% Jun 17)**, **VXN 28.56 (+5.97% Jun 17)** — VXN crush expected on event clarity did NOT occur; vol repriced HIGHER on hawkish reset. 10-pt VIX/VXN gap persists. SKEW/VVIX/term structure not retrievable |
| 3. Macro / Liquidity | **-2** | Strongly bearish | **FOMC median 2026 dot raised 3.4% → 3.8%; 9 of 18 see ≥1 hike, 6 see multiple; statement stripped easing bias; Warsh declined own dot.** 2Y +16bp to **4.21% (year-high)**. 10Y 4.463. DXY +0.74% to 100.28 (back above par). HY OAS **278 bp** (widened from 271 bp; still tight). May CPI 4.2% YoY backdrop persists |
| 4. Breadth / Internals | **0** | Neutral | Jun 17: **SPX -1.21%, NDX -0.99%, Nasdaq Comp -1.34%, DJI -0.98%, RUT -0.72%** — broad-front decline of ~1% across all major indexes. No leadership, no rotation refuge. Internals detail (A/D, McClellan, % >50DMA) not retrievable |
| 5. Cross-Asset | **+1** | Bullish (held) | Brent **$78.41** stable (Iran-relief premium digested); Gold **$4,341.20** holding (+0.24%); DXY 100.28 (mild strength but not disruptive); HY OAS 278 bp tight. Cross-asset still consistent with "Iran-relief + tight-credit" regime, not a hawkish-Fed crisis |
| 6. Sentiment | **-1** | Bearish (drift) | **F&G 39.5** (down from 41.29 Jun 16, 40 Jun 17) — never confirmed Jun 15 rally and now drifting lower into a hawkish Fed; momentum is south, not catching a bid post-event |
| 7. Geopolitical | **+2** | Bullish (held) | **MOU formal signing Jun 19 Geneva** on track per CSIS, Global Guardian, NBC, CNN, Al Jazeera (VP Vance + Speaker Ghalibaf). 60-day ceasefire framework + Hormuz reopened. Israel halted attacks. No Trump escalation Jun 17-18. Lebanon clause is residual tail |
| **TOTAL** | **-1** | — | Net mildly negative — macro headwind + dealer-flow repricing dominate; cross-asset + geopolitical relief cushion |

## Diverging Signals
- **The cleanest divergence is Geopolitical/Credit (+3 combined) vs Macro/Dealer (-3 combined).** The Fed gave markets a hawkish reset on the same week Iran delivered a peace framework. The macro lens says "tighter financial conditions ahead"; the geopolitical lens says "tail risk removed from the equity discount." Which dominates near-term: the macro headwind, because it's now in the price and dot plot, while Iran relief is largely digested.
- **VXN at 28.56 alongside VIX at 18.44 (gap of ~10 pts) widened, not narrowed.** Markets are pricing NDX-specific tail risk higher post-FOMC, while SPX vol remains contained. Asymmetric: NDX is where the rates pain shows up first (long-duration growth, leveraged tech), and dealers are not letting VXN compress.
- **F&G drifted Fear-er (41.29 → 40 → 39.5) into a tape that's only -1.2% on the week.** Sentiment is sliding faster than price; either price catches down or sentiment rebounds. The hawkish FOMC tilts the asymmetry toward the former.
- **Warsh structurally shortened the Fed's communication footprint** (curt statement, fewer pressers, no own-dot, no forward guidance). That removes a market-stabilizing mechanism — events between meetings now matter more, and surprise prints (Philly Fed today, jobs/CPI ahead) carry more reflexivity. Net: vol-of-vol structurally higher.
- **2Y at 4.21% (16bp move, year-high) is a real-economy signal**, not just a tape move. The front-end is repricing the entire rate path. Until the curve digests this, equity multiples carry an extra discount factor.

## -- TQQQ INSTRUMENT ANALYSIS --

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI(14) reported at **69.04 (Barchart)** but that reading was likely pre-close intraday at $80.95; with Jun 17 ending ~-3.0%, post-close RSI estimated **~60-65 — back to neutral**. Two-day down streak (Jun 16 -5.27%, Jun 17 ~-3.0%). Momentum reset out of overbought, no oversold tailwind yet.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS** (held). Estimated spot $77.7: -11.8% from 4-wk high $88.09 (above the -20% DOWNTURN threshold) AND +14.1% above 4-wk low $68.08 (below the +30% RALLY threshold). DOWNTURN trigger requires close ≤ $70.47 (-9.3% from spot) AND VXN ≥ 25 — the VXN condition IS already met at 28.56, so a ~9% further drop activates DOWNTURN. RALLY trigger requires close ≥ $88.50 (+13.9% from spot).
- **Trend:** Price still above both MAs but 50DMA cushion compressed to ~+1.7% (from +4.9% Jun 16, +10.7% Jun 15). Two-MA uptrend intact in name only — one ordinary down day breaks it. 200DMA cushion still healthy at +14.1%.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN 28.56** says event-priced richness DID NOT drain (the bullish-trigger scenario); instead it modestly re-fattened on hawkish-Fed repricing. Premium is structurally richer than late Stage 1, but the richness now reflects realized-risk repricing rather than event-window overhang. Expected move widened to ±12.1% with the EM lower bound retesting the 4-wk low / 200DMA confluence at $68 — a one-sigma down week reaches the next major support.
- **Put-selling context: CAUTIOUS** (held — but degraded edge). Five reasons: (1) **Macro lens decisively bearish** post-FOMC — 2Y year-high 4.21%, 9-of-18 dot-plot escalation, easing bias removed, persistent headwind for leveraged growth. (2) **TQQQ sits +1.7% above 50DMA $76.39** — one ordinary down day from the bearish daily-close trigger; the structural support buffer is essentially gone. (3) **VXN 28.56 + EM ±12.1% means premium is rich but range is wide** — the one-sigma downside $68.3 retests the 4-wk low / 200DMA confluence. (4) **Quad-witching + MOU signing stack tomorrow Jun 19** — structural pin-unwind + delta-hedging + geopolitical execution all in one session; reflexivity elevated. (5) **F&G drifted Fear-er to 39.5** with momentum south — no sentiment cushion. *Counter:* Credit holding (HY OAS 278 bp tight), Iran MOU on track (geopolitical tail removed), Cross-Asset stable, VIX 18.44 still inside 15-22 range (not Stage 4 panic), and the 200DMA cushion is +14.1% (real structural support). **Net: CAUTIOUS not HOSTILE — premium-to-risk is defensible but path-risk is elevated, and a daily 50DMA break (only -1.7%) would flip the read to HOSTILE in real-time.** Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (back to Stage 2):** TQQQ daily close back above $80.13 (reclaim Jun 16 high), AND VXN crushes through 22, AND a clean MOU signing Jun 19 + Philly Fed Jun 18 not so hot it reinforces hawkish narrative, AND no follow-on hawkish Fed-speak in the next 5 sessions, AND F&G back through 45.
- **Bearish trigger (to Stage 4):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39 (only -1.7% from spot), OR VIX sustains above 22 for 2+ sessions, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR a hot Philly Fed Jun 18 + low jobless claims combo that reinforces hawkish tape, OR MOU collapse / Israel re-engagement pre-Geneva, OR Trump tariff escalation post.
- **Neutralizer:** A clean week with no incremental hawkish Fed-speak (Warsh now declines pressers between meetings — structurally fewer comm events) + Philly Fed and CPI/PCE prints that re-anchor a soft-landing narrative; OR a tech mega-cap earnings (Accenture/Kroger Jun 18; then late-June Micron / late-July MSFT/GOOG/META/AAPL) that re-establishes growth leadership.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 18, 2026 (TODAY):** **Philly Fed Manufacturing 8:30 ET** (forecast +11.4 vs prior -0.4); **Initial Jobless Claims 8:30 ET**; **Accenture (ACN), Kroger (KR) earnings.**
- **Jun 19, 2026 (Fri):** **US-Iran MOU formal Geneva signing (VP Vance + Speaker Ghalibaf)**; **June Quad-Witching Expiry** (options + futures + index + single-stock).
- **Jun 22, 2026 (Mon):** First post-quad / post-MOU tape day.
- **Late June: Micron Technology (MU) earnings** — first major Q2-tape semis read.
- **Jun 30, 2026 (Tue):** Quarter-end rebalance — last business day of Q2.
- **Jul 3, 2026 (Fri):** NFP June payrolls.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 18 Thu today, Jun 19 Fri, Jun 22 Mon, Jun 23 Tue, Jun 24 Wed): **Philly Fed + Initial Claims + ACN/KR earnings Jun 18**; **MOU formal Geneva signing + June Quad-Witching Jun 19**; first post-event tape Jun 22; possible Micron earnings tail.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — geopolitical-execution event + structural expiry within 2 sessions; data prints today with implications for the Fed path; recent FOMC volatility still digesting.

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Next is **Jun 19, 2026** (3rd Friday). Days-until: **1 trading day.** **WITHIN 2 SESSIONS.** Elevated volume + pinning + delta-hedge unwind dynamics dominate the session; coincident with MOU signing and first post-FOMC week-close — Jun 19 stacking remains structurally exceptional.
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Next is **Jun 30, 2026** (last business day of Q2). Days-until: **~8 trading days.** Not within 2 sessions, but inside the broader mid-to-late June expiry-rebalance complex.
- **Net:** **Jun 19 stays structurally exceptional** — quad-witching + MOU formal signing + post-FOMC week-close converge. Intraday vol/volume structurally elevated; pinning behavior will be unusual; short-put fills behave differently than a typical Friday. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook

- **Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing — Jun 18, 2026 8:30 ET (forecast +11.4 vs prior -0.4, post-hawkish-FOMC tape).** This is a data print, not a decision event, but in a freshly-hawkish regime it carries asymmetric reflexivity. Two named historical anchors:
  - **Philly Fed Jul 17, 2008 (regional-deterioration regime):** Print missed badly (-16.3 vs -8.0 expected); SPX -1.1% on day, accelerated existing weakness. Lesson: in a regime already pricing tight policy, big data misses one-way down.
  - **Philly Fed Apr 18, 2024 (hot-inflation regime):** Beat headline (+15.5 vs +1.5 expected); 10Y +5bp on day, SPX -0.22%, growth-tech underperformed. Lesson: in a hot-inflation hawkish-Fed regime, a hot beat reinforces tighter-for-longer and hurts leveraged growth specifically.
  - **Net alert:** A meaningful Philly Fed beat today (≥ +11.4) reinforces the hawkish FOMC tape and adds pressure to TQQQ-relevant rate-sensitive names. A meaningful miss (<+5) gives the Fed cover to soften posture and could anchor a relief bounce. The reaction asymmetry now leans negative (hawkish-Fed already locked in; hot-data confirms; cool-data partially relieves).

- **US-Iran MOU formal Geneva signing — Jun 19, 2026 (combined with June quad-witching).** Historical signing-day anchors: **Camp David Accords Sep 1978** (SPX +0.7%, framework already priced); **Israel-Egypt Treaty Mar 1979** (markets flat); **JCPOA Jul 14, 2015** (SPX +0.1%, oil -2.6% — framework already priced). Pattern: **formal signing is rarely the catalyst when the framework is already public.** Combined with quad-witching: structural pin-unwind + delta hedging amplify any directional move. Residual tails: (a) Lebanon clause (Hezbollah/Iran want Israeli withdrawal as ceasefire predicate); (b) any Iran walk-back of a specific MOU term; (c) Trump escalation post pre-ceremony. Brent already digested most of the relief premium ($78.41 from $90+ pre-MOU).

- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ through this window means accepting (1) hawkish-Fed macro headwind that is now structurally in the price path (median 2026 dot 3.8%, 9-of-18 hike posture), (2) thin 50DMA cushion of ~+1.7% — a single ordinary down day breaks it and flips the read to HOSTILE, (3) Jun 19 quad-witching + MOU signing structural elevation, (4) two-day down streak in spot (Jun 16 -5.27%, Jun 17 est. ~-3.0%) with momentum still south, (5) VXN at 28.56 (sticky high, not crushing) with EM lower bound at $68.3 testing the 4-wk low / 200DMA confluence in a one-sigma move. Cushion items: credit tight (HY OAS 278), Iran framework executing tomorrow, 200DMA still +14.1% below spot, VIX in Stage 3 range. Historical hawkish-FOMC-into-event-week episodes (Jun 2022, Sep 2022) saw 3-5% follow-through downside the following week. **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - Analysis only. No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - **TQQQ Jun 17 close not retrievable** from public search at time of read; ~$77.7 marked clearly as an estimate derived from the NDX -0.99% session move (3x leverage approximation) and validated against Barchart's MA5 of $81.33. All TQQQ table values dependent on the estimate are tagged accordingly.
> - VIX/VXN values from live snapshot block, used verbatim.
> - 2Y +16bp to 4.21% sourced from CNN Business FOMC recap; 2s10s shown as approximation given 10Y 4.463 from snapshot.
> - VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M term structure, SKEW, VVIX, MOVE index, S&P % above 50DMA, NAAIM/AAII/COT detail, TQQQ Greeks, exact put/call ratio, and option-chain implied vols remain unavailable from public search and are marked accordingly.
> - TQQQ IV / 5-day EM clearly labeled as estimates with VXN-input dependence.
> - Philly Fed Jun 18 actual print not yet available in search; treated as a forward catalyst with playbook for both beat / miss outcomes.
> - Conservative scoring applied: Dealer Flow downgraded to -1 because VXN did NOT crush despite event clarity (vol crush bullish trigger failed); Macro -2 because 2Y at year-high + 9-of-18 hike-posture is a multi-month regime signal, not a single print; Breadth held at 0 (not -1) because the decline was orderly, not capitulatory.
> - Stage transition 2 → 3 with Stage 4 trigger ARMED; Confidence Medium because the 50DMA gate is one ordinary down day away.
